data.narrative:
Modern Intelligence is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by John Dulin, Joe Cieslik, and Tristan Tager, having raised $5.12M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and stage proximity, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $5.12M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $13M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, producing a median valuation of $300M (range: $60M–$29.0B), with the wide spread reflecting heterogeneity in defense-tech funding and scale outcomes.

The combined triangulation supports a range of $11M to $345M, with a midpoint of $156M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound at $13M; the peer-median method dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven by scale AI's $29B outlier and early-stage Tenna Systems at $60M—and the absence of recent comparable Seed-stage exits in defense tech. A valuation toward the lower-to-middle range ($11M–$50M) aligns more conservatively with Seed fundamentals, while higher multiples would require evidence of outsized traction or differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
